verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine with no intrinsic TM domain, but it reaches the outer face of the plasma membrane via two documented, cell-state-dependent mechanisms: (1) The latent TGFB1 small latent complex (SLC, LAP+mature TGF-β1) is covalently tethered to the transmembrane protein GARP (LRRC32) or LRRC33 via a disulfide bond, stably anchoring it to the cell surface of activated Tregs, B cells, platelets, and myeloid cells. This is a co-trafficked, wash-resistant complex — the protein body reaches the outer leaflet as part of this complex. (2) Surface LAP/TGF-β1 is also documented on Foxp3+ Tregs via GRP78-dependent anchoring. Both mechanisms are cell-state-induced (requiring cellular activation). Multiple therapeutic mAbs and bispecifics explicitly target the GARP:TGF-β1 complex on the cell surface, confirming external accessibility. The dominant form circulates as a soluble/ECM-tethered large latent complex, so baseline localization is not constitutively surface; surface presentation is restricted to specific activated cell types and states.
